name: research
description: v3.0.0 - Proposition-first R&D protocol with observation/analysis state, derived hypothesis plans, plan review, result analysis, and proposition feedback.

interface.shortDescription: Proposition-first R&D protocol with plan review and result analysis
interface.longDescription: v3.0.0 - A proposition-first protocol skill for agent-driven R&D across the three OECD Frascati Manual 2015 categories: basic_research, applied_research, and experimental_development. The top-level research unit is a proposition, not a standalone plan. Research starts from material such as observations, failures, constraints, comparators, traces, prior-work facts, theoretical tensions, or search/evaluation bottlenecks; analysis then records a contrast, Generated doubt, Working proposition, Expected consequence, observed match/break/missing condition, Proposition status, and only then a derived hypothesis when the status permits one. The project layout stores proposition state under propositions/Pxxx_slug/ with observations.md, analyses.md, decisions.md, and hypotheses/Hxxx_slug/ ledgers. Hypothesis plans live under the derived hypothesis and test one hypothesis traced to the parent proposition and source analysis. Material absence creates a material-acquisition task, not a proposition or hypothesis. Contradicted, under-specified, split-needed, split, and closed proposition states block hypothesis-plan creation until the proposition is revised, split, supplied with more material, routed to children, or closed. Plans retain pre-result commitments including proposition trace, hypothesis type, prediction or expected observation, primary measure, comparator/control when required, evidence route, artifacts, material conditions, Plan visual, and stop/status criteria. research-plan-review remains the plan-path-only pre-execution stop gate for wrong or unsupported premises, invalid validation methods, missing proposition trace, weak Plan visuals, and ungrounded prior-work survey evidence. research-result-analysis remains the plan-path-only post-execution analysis skill: it reconstructs evidence, explains what happened and why through candidate explanations, evidence for/against, procedure/artifact explanations, and live alternatives, but does not write final claims or choose proposition or hypothesis decisions. After result analysis, the parent workflow updates hypothesis.md, hypothesis decisions, parent proposition.md, and proposition decisions before opening any next hypothesis. Methods reproducibility is enforced through material conditions and durable artifacts; experiment-level replicability infrastructure remains discretionary. Reports remain paper-grade evidence artifacts under derived hypotheses with required Related Work, Theory/Formulation, Methods/Conditions, Results/Observations, Ablation/Sensitivity, Discussion, Limitations, and References sections, without next-action or next-hypothesis queues.
defaultPrompt: Design an R&D plan for this investigation
Help me decide what to do after this experimental result
Write a human-readable report from this plan and runs
